input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

In the bottom right corner, a dotted grid logo containing letters sits next to a "gt." logo with a red dot. The image remains unchanged throughout the duration.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A static white title screen displays the word "Shaving" in large, dark grey sans-serif font. Beneath it, smaller text reads "High Speed Video Microscope Footage". The scene appears static with no significant movement or changes observed in this interval as the shaver remains pressed against the beard.

The corner logos, including 'gt.', remain visible in the bottom right. The scene exhibits no state changes or user interactions throughout this duration.

In the bottom right corner, a grid of dots and the text "gt." remain visible. The scene is entirely motionless; the shaver stays fixed against the facial hair without any movement, state changes, or interactions occurring throughout the duration. The lighting and composition remain constant, emphasizing the texture of the stubble and the mechanical details of the shaver head.

queries.question: Describe the key visual events occurring during the shaving demonstration from start to finish.

answers.text: The video begins with a static title card reading "Shaving" and "High Speed Video Microscope Footage" until the 4-second mark. At 4 seconds, the scene shifts to a black-and-white close-up of a chin with coarse stubble on the left. An electric shaver head, featuring exposed white internal components and a diagram label, presses directly against the facial hair. This high-contrast view remains largely static through the 10-second mark, with logos persisting in the corner.

samples.output: <think>The static macro perspective continues, with a circular spotlight highlighting the shaver head pressing into coarse stubble. White internal components and a labeled attachment stay centered against a black void. No motion or lighting changes occur; the scene remains a frozen, high-contrast close-up of the shaving mechanism interacting with facial hair.</think><answer></answer>

queries.question: Describe the key visual events occurring during the shaving demonstration from start to finish.

This high-contrast view remains largely static through the 10-second mark, with logos persisting in the corner.

From 12 seconds onward, the visual content remains entirely static without any new events. The high-contrast black-and-white close-up continues to show the chin with coarse stubble pressed firmly against the electric shaver head. The device's exposed white internal components, inverted labels, and corner logos persist unchanged.
